# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

# Install necessary packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    docker.io \
    curl \
    gnupg \
    lsb-release

# Set up Docker daemon configuration to allow root user interaction
RUN mkdir /etc/docker/daemon.d \
    && echo '{"userns-remap":"root"}' > /etc/docker/daemon.d/userns.conf \
    && systemctl restart docker

# Give the container elevated permissions
USER root

# Install additional tools for container orchestration
RUN apt-get install -y \
    jq \
    python3 \
    python3-pip

# Install Python libraries
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir docker-compose

# Set environment variables for Docker
ENV DOCKER_HOST="unix:///var/run/docker.sock"

# Copy local scripts or configurations if needed
# COPY . /app
# WORKDIR /app

# Command to run the orchestration service
CMD ["python3", "orchestrate.py"]
